title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 14 - Temple of Shadows Expansion
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G2COlj5SS54

Daniel (@dungeondive) tackles the Temple of Shadows, a peculiar expansion that functions as Forbidden Fortress's second core set — or, as a cynical gamer might say, "DLC that was already on the disc." Rather than a new otherworld, it augments the base game with four heroes, four enemy types, new map tiles, and cards that shuffle into existing decks.

The four heroes are a mixed bag. The Soldier is a straightforward brawler, the Geisha feels like a reskinned Saloon Girl (same Dainty and Showman abilities), and the Enforcer is another melee fighter with impressive Endurance 4. The standout is the Kitsune — a fox spirit warrior with stealth, the Mythic keyword, and elemental magic access. Daniel wishes Flying Frog had leaned harder into Japanese mythology, pitching a "yokai master" hero who could summon captured spirits to fight alongside them.

Enemies include chochanobake lantern yokai (explosive on death), skin crawlers (single hits dealing up to 12 damage on brutal side), takobake spearmen (tentacle-wrapped 15-foot spears with 3-space melee range), and the fallen shogun — a mini-boss with a sweeping brutal blade and dark stone armour. Adventure theme cards add replayable modifiers to any mission. Daniel recommends this expansion only if Forbidden Fortress is your sole Brimstone purchase, noting it's hard to justify otherwise given the relatively thin contents for a "deluxe" label.

----

Should Shadows of Brimstone lean more into Japanese mythology and yokai for future Forbidden Fortress content?
